title: Psalms, Chapter 3
description: Psalms chapter 3. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 88. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 88.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>ψαλμοϲ τω δαυειδ οποτε απεδιδραϲκεν απο προϲωπου αβεϲϲαλωμ του υιου αυτου <sup>2</sup>κε τι επληθυνθηϲαν οι θλιβοντεϲ με πολλοι επανιϲτανται επ εμε <sup>3</sup>πολλοι λεγουϲιν τη ψυχη μου ουκ εϲτιν ϲωτηρια αυτω εν τω θω αυτου · διαψαλμα <sup>4</sup>ϲυ δε κε αντιλημπτωρ μου ει δοξα μου και ϋψων την κεφαλην μου <sup>5</sup>φωνη μου προϲ κν εκεκραξα και επηκουϲεν μου εξ ορουϲ αγιου αυτου διαψαλμα
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>LORD, how are they increased that trouble me! many
are they that rise up against me. <sup>2</sup>Many
there be which say of my soul,
There is no help for him in God. Selah. <sup>3</sup>But thou, O LORD,
art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head. <sup>4</sup>I cried unto the LORD with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah. <sup>5</sup>I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>εγω εκοιμηθην και ϋπνωϲα εξηγερθην οτι κϲ αντελαβετο μου <sup>7</sup>ου φοβηθηϲομαι απο μυριαδων λαου των κυκλω ϲυνεπιτιθεμενων μοι <sup>8</sup>αναϲτα κε ϲωϲον με ο θϲ μου οτι ϲυ επαταξαϲ πανταϲ τουϲ εχθραινονταϲ μοι ματαιωϲ οδονταϲ αμαρτωλων ϲυνετριψαϲ <sup>9</sup>του κυ η ϲωτηρια και επι τον λαον ϲου η ευλογια ϲου
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that have set
themselves against me round about. <sup>7</sup>Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies
upon the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly. <sup>8</sup>Salvation
belongeth unto the LORD: thy blessing
is
 upon thy people. Selah.
